assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as an admission webhook issue and states 'This is a common issue after Kubernetes upgrades due to API version changes or webhook configuration problems'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response provides both 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks' commands to identify webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 1 which explicitly offers to disable the webhook as the fastest solution

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes specific delete commands: 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While the response mentions it as a 'quick fix' and labels it as 'fastest', it doesn't explicitly warn that deleting the webhook removes validation or emphasize it's temporary

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes checking current version and upgrading to a version compatible with k8s 1.32 in the permanent fix section

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response provides helm upgrade commands and mentions upgrading to 'latest version compatible with k8s 1.32' as the permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention failurePolicy or setting it to Ignore as an alternative to webhook deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never -- echo "success"' in the verify section

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not mention persisting changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Response does not discuss failurePolicy: Ignore at all, so cannot qualify it as temporary or discuss reverting to Fail
